items.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
items.summary: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

items.title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
items.summary: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

items.title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
items.summary: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

items.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
items.summary: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

items.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
items.summary: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

items.title: ‘Too early’ to talk IPO, Redwood Materials’ incoming CFO says
items.summary: The battery-recycling and energy-storage company has hired former Tesla finance chief Deepak Ahuja, reuniting him with former Tesla CTO JB Straubel.

items.title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
items.summary: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

items.title: TikTok launches an ad-free subscription plan in the UK
items.summary: Users who sign up for the plan won’t see ads on TikTok, and their data won’t be used for advertising purposes.

items.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
items.summary: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

items.title: Discord launches Nitro Rewards, giving subscribers access to the base tier of Xbox Game Pass for no extra cost
items.summary: Discord's Nitro Rewards program will give Nitro subscribers access to Xbox Game Pass and discounts from Logitech, SteelSeries, and other gaming brands.

items.title: Venmo’s biggest makeover in years comes at a very interesting time
items.summary: The timing is notable. PayPal, which owns Venmo, is restructuring to spin Venmo off as a stand-alone business unit — a move widely seen as laying the groundwork for a potential sale. Stripe has reportedly expressed interest in buying PayPal outright.

items.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
items.summary: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

items.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
items.summary: Production-version patches are coming online and should be installed pronto.

items.title: Audi has a new Q9 flagship coming soon: Here's its interior
items.summary: Audi made sure to consult American tastes for its first full-size SUV.

items.title: After banning foreign routers, FCC says existing ones can get updates until 2029
items.summary: FCC extends waiver allowing routers and drones to get patches for two more years.

items.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months
items.summary: Can AI save us from the AI industry’s endless thirst for water? Outlook not so good.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship arrive in US; 3 people in biocontainment
items.summary: A US passenger tested "mildly positive," but WHO is calling it "inconclusive" for now.

items.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
items.summary: Shutdown of Starlink location feature won’t dampen interest in GPS alternatives.

items.title: iOS, macOS, and iPadOS 26.5 updates arrive with encrypted RCS messaging and more
items.summary: Probably the last big updates we'll see before the next versions appear at WWDC.

items.title: Samsung made a “mockery” of Dua Lipa by putting her picture on TV boxes, lawsuit says
items.summary: Lipa says her picture contributed to Samsung's "enormous revenue."
